title: Article REST API Endpoints

story.i_want_to: manage knowledge articles through REST API endpoints
story.so_that: agents can create articles from findings and administrators can curate the knowledge wiki via API

rationale: The Knowledge context module provides the domain logic, but agents and administrators need HTTP endpoints to interact with it. The Article REST API exposes CRUD operations with appropriate role-based access control -- agents can create and read articles (capturing findings during implementation loops), while users/admins have full update and delete capabilities. Project-scoped routes enable agents to work within their assigned project context without needing to specify project_id in every request body.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/articles creates a tenant-wide article. Requires role: agent or higher. Request body includes title, body, category, and optional fields (status, tags, source_type, source_id, metadata). Returns 201 with the created article.

POST /api/v1/projects/:project_id/articles creates a project-scoped article. The project_id from the URL path is set on the article. Requires role: agent or higher. Returns 201 with the created article.

GET /api/v1/articles lists all articles for the authenticated tenant. Supports query params: category, status, tags (comma-separated), limit, offset. Requires role: agent or higher. Returns 200 with paginated article list.

GET /api/v1/projects/:project_id/articles lists articles scoped to a specific project. Supports the same query params as the tenant-wide list. Requires role: agent or higher. Returns 200 with paginated article list.
GET /api/v1/articles/:id returns a single article with its outgoing and incoming links preloaded. Requires role: agent or higher. Returns 200 with the article or 404 if not found.

PATCH /api/v1/articles/:id updates an article. Requires role: user or higher (agents cannot update). Accepts partial updates to title, body, category, status, tags, metadata. Returns 200 with the updated article.
DELETE /api/v1/articles/:id archives an article (sets status to :archived, does not hard delete). Requires role: user or higher. Returns 200 with the archived article.
Controller uses `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for consistent error rendering. Uses RequireRole plug for role-based access control.
Routes are added to router.ex under the authenticated API pipeline (/api/v1/). Both tenant-wide and project-scoped routes are registered.
JSON responses use a consistent structure with article fields serialized. Links in the show response include id, relationship_type, and the linked article's id and title.
All endpoints enforce tenant isolation -- a request authenticated as tenant A cannot access tenant B's articles. Cross-tenant access returns 404.

New error atoms used by the Knowledge context (:empty_query, :invalid_search_mode, :invalid_supersession_target) are either added to FallbackController or the context uses existing atoms with the 3-tuple pattern {:error, :bad_request, message}. Prefer the 3-tuple pattern to avoid modifying FallbackController.

test_cases.name: Create tenant-wide article via POST /api/v1/articles
steps: POST /api/v1/articles with body {"title": "Pattern: Config-based DI", "body": "Use behaviours + Application.get_env...", "category": "pattern", "tags": ["elixir", "di"]}
expected_results: Response status is 201
Response body has article with id, title, category='pattern', status='draft', tags=['elixir', 'di']
Article has no project_id (tenant-wide)
Article exists in database with correct tenant_id

test_cases.name: Create project-scoped article via POST /api/v1/projects/:project_id/articles
steps: POST /api/v1/projects/:project_id/articles with body {"title": "Convention: snake_case modules", "body": "All modules use snake_case...", "category": "convention"}
expected_results: Response status is 201
Response body has article with project_id matching the URL param
Article exists in database with correct tenant_id and project_id

test_cases.name: List articles with filtering by category and tags
expected_results: Response status is 200
Returns only the article matching both category=pattern AND tags containing 'elixir'
Response includes pagination metadata

test_cases.name: Show article includes preloaded links
preconditions: fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, source_article_id: article_a.id, target_article_id: article_b.id, relationship_type: :relates_to})
expected_results: Response status is 200
Response includes outgoing_links array with the relates_to link to article_b
Each link includes id, relationship_type, and linked article id and title

test_cases.name: Agent role cannot update or delete articles
expected_results: Both PATCH and DELETE return 403 Forbidden (agent role insufficient for user-level endpoints)

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: cross-tenant article access returns 404
expected_results: Response is 404 Not Found
Tenant A's article is not visible to Tenant B

test_cases.name: Delete (archive) article sets status to archived
expected_results: Response status is 200
Article status is :archived in the response and database
Article record still exists (not hard deleted)

test_cases.name: Invalid article creation returns 422 with errors
expected_results: Response status is 422 Unprocessable Entity
Response body includes validation errors for title, body, and category

technical_notes: Controller: `defmodule LoopctlWeb.ArticleController do use LoopctlWeb, :controller`. Use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController`.
Role enforcement: `plug LoopctlWeb.Plugs.RequireRole, :agent when action in [:create, :index, :show]` and `plug LoopctlWeb.Plugs.RequireRole, :user when action in [:update, :delete]`.

Routes in router.ex under authenticated pipeline: `resources "/articles", ArticleController, except: [:new, :edit]` for tenant-wide, and nested `resources "/articles", ArticleController, only: [:create, :index]` under `/projects/:project_id` for project-scoped.
The create action for project-scoped route should merge project_id from conn.params into the article attrs before calling Knowledge.create_article/3.

JSON view should serialize: id, tenant_id, project_id, title, body, category, status, tags, source_type, source_id, metadata, inserted_at, updated_at. For show action, also include outgoing_links and incoming_links.
Tags query param should be comma-separated in the URL: ?tags=elixir,otp. Parse to list in the controller before passing to context.
All list endpoints MUST use consistent pagination: limit/offset with defaults (limit: 20, max: 100).

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs in DataCase/ConnCase setup, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex, (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests.
JSON rendering via LoopctlWeb.ArticleJSON module (Phoenix 1.8 pattern, not legacy View). Define article(%{article: article}) and index(%{articles: articles, meta: meta}) functions.
